trivia_bullets.text: Theros Beyond Death (THB) was released January 24, 2020 as the third set in the Theros block family. THB returned to the Greek-mythology-themed plane and emphasized the underworld of Nyx, escape mechanics, and mortal arena-combat culture. The Arena Trickster's flavor text captures Theros's theatrical gladiatorial spectacle: 'It's not enough to surprise your opponent. You have to delight the crowd as well.'
sources: Theros Beyond Death set page via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Arena Trickster is a {3}{R} 3/3 Human Shaman with a flash-responsive trigger: whenever you cast your first spell during each opponent's turn, it gains a +1/+1 counter. EDHREC tracks it in approximately 320 decks (0.01% of all) — functional in Standard-era draft where flash spells were common, but too slow for most EDH contexts.
sources: EDHREC: Arena Trickster via WebSearch snippet
MTGGoldfish: Theros Beyond Death Instant-Speed Tricks via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Illustrated by PINDURSKI, a Polish digital artist with multiple MTG set credits. The Arena Trickster composition — red-lit stone archway, theatrical mask, mid-laugh performer — uses a high-saturation dramatic-figure approach characteristic of PINDURSKI's work.

On Theros, arena combat is explicitly public entertainment tied to religious devotion: the gods watch and grant boons to warriors who earn crowd approval. The theatrical mask the figure holds is a performance prop rather than protective gear, underlining the performance-over-function ethos of the Theros arena tradition.
sources: MTG Wiki: Theros via WebSearch snippet
